test.name: Linking an assertion to a tree person should route to person-evidence, not tree-edit
test.description: Per the SKILL.md description's explicit `Do NOT use` clause: 'Do NOT use when the user wants to link assertions to persons (use person-evidence).' The user has an existing assertion in research.json and wants to associate it with a tree person via person_evidence — that's a research.json write, not a tree edit. tree-edit operates on tree.gedcomx.json (persons, facts, relationships); person_evidence operates on the research.json person_evidence section.

input.user_message: Create a new person_evidence record linking assertion a_002 (Patrick Flynn birth ~1845, Ireland — from the 1850 census) to person I1. I want a person_evidence entry added to research.json that ties this assertion to Patrick.

negative.explanation: Creating or updating a person_evidence link between an assertion and a tree person is person-evidence's job — it writes to the research.json person_evidence section. tree-edit writes to tree.gedcomx.json. The skill description is explicit: 'Do NOT use when the user wants to link assertions to persons (use person-evidence).'

judge_context: Should route to person-evidence — the user is asking to create a person_evidence link, which is person-evidence's responsibility
Should NOT make any edit to tree.gedcomx.json — assertion-to-person links are in research.json, not the tree file
May briefly explain the difference between tree-edit (writes to tree.gedcomx.json: persons, facts, relationships) and person-evidence (writes to research.json's person_evidence section)
